Paragraph

  1. Does each equivalence class of Cauchy sequences \(\{ c_{k}\}\) in \(C[a, b]\) under the \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm associate to a pointwise-defined function on \([a, b]\text{?}\)
  2. Does each equivalence class of Cauchy sequences \(\{ c_{k}\}\) in \(C[a, b]\) under the \(L^{p}[a, b]\)-norm associate to a well-defined integral? What usual properties of integrals are preserved?
  3. Establish criteria for other limiting process of functions in \(C[a, b]\) or \(L^{p}[a, b]\text{,}\) e.g., pointwise limit, that result in a limit in \(L^{p}[a, b]\text{.}\)
in-context
